title: Lesson 36 — The Judge

words.definition: treating all sides equally; not biased
words.example: An impartial referee is essential to a fair match.

words.definition: showing no prejudice for or against something; fair
words.example: The study used an unbiased sample of participants.

words.definition: fair and impartial, especially in the distribution of something
words.example: The settlement aimed for an equitable split of assets.

words.definition: not influenced by personal feelings; based on facts
words.example: An objective account of the accident, free of blame.

words.definition: strongly biased in favor of one side or party
words.example: The vote split along strictly partisan lines.

words.definition: having a preconceived opinion not based on reason or experience
words.example: A prejudiced juror should have been dismissed.

words.definition: a tendency to favor one side; prejudice
words.example: The editor tried to correct for his own bias.

words.definition: to treat differently, especially unfairly, on some basis; also, to distinguish carefully
words.example: The law forbids employers to discriminate by age.

words.definition: not influenced by strong emotion; calm and rational
words.example: A dispassionate analysis set aside all the outrage.

words.definition: to reach a decision as an impartial referee between disputing parties
words.example: A retired judge was asked to arbitrate the dispute.

words.definition: to make a formal judgment on a disputed matter
words.example: The panel will adjudicate all appeals by Friday.

words.definition: to intervene between disputing parties to bring about an agreement
words.example: A neutral counselor was brought in to mediate.

story: The two neighbors needed someone truly [[impartial|impartial]] to settle the fence dispute, someone [[unbiased|unbiased]] enough that both would trust the outcome and reach an [[equitable|equitable]] result. They chose old Mrs. Alcorn, known across the block for her strictly [[objective|objective]] view of every quarrel; unlike the [[partisan|partisan]] gossip that usually followed such fights, she was never [[prejudiced|prejudiced]] toward either side, and refused to let personal [[bias|bias]] [[discriminate|discriminate]] between the two families. Cool and [[dispassionate|dispassionate]], she agreed to [[arbitrate|arbitrate]] the matter, [[adjudicate|adjudicate]] the property line herself, and [[mediate|mediate]] whatever argument followed.
